title: Ignore Files With .gitignore
description:
## Keep Junk and Secrets Out of Git

A repository is ready at **`/root/ignorerepo`** containing `app.py`, `secret.env`, and `debug.log`.

**Your task:** create a **`.gitignore`** in that repo so that:
1. **all `*.log`** files are ignored
2. the file **`secret.env`** is ignored

> 💡 Each line of `.gitignore` is a pattern (e.g. `*.log`, `secret.env`). Verify with `git check-ignore <file>` — it echoes the path when a file is ignored.

hints.title: Write the ignore patterns
hints.body: Add one pattern per line. *.log matches any log file; a bare filename matches that file.

hints.title: Verify
hints.body: git check-ignore prints the path of any file that a .gitignore rule matches.

validation.description: Checks that secret.env and *.log files are ignored in /root/ignorerepo.
